metadata.description: Retrospective logging of unlogged decisions and contributions from March 21-26, 2026 conversation: repo restructuring, em dash removal, DOI completion, 3-way comparison, NatureCollection2026 venue targeting, cover letter and editor inquiry drafting.

summary: Retrospective logging of 7 events from the March 21-26 conversation that were not logged in real time: (1) repo restructuring contribution, (2) 3-way PCA comparison contribution, (3) em dash removal decision, (4) DOI completion contribution, (5) Nature Collection venue targeting decision, (6) NatureCollection2026 submission package contribution, (7) repository structure state change. Combined with the 2 previously logged sessions (trace_20260321: PCA methodology, trace_20260326_04ea82: citation style + precision), this brings the total logged events for this conversation to 17 across 3 sessions.

contribution.description: Complete GitHub repository restructuring: created src/ package (8 modules with Pydantic data models), tests/ (114 passing tests), notebooks/ (3 clean notebooks), data/ (split corpus + artist perspectives + Likert anchors), supplementary/ (Tables S1-S3 + consensus UMAP implementation details), trace_provenance/ (25 session files), scripts/ (PCA comparison + 3-way analysis), figures/, pyproject.toml, .gitignore. Replaced the previous 5-file repo with 40+ new files organized for public release.

context.conversation_snippet: User: 'i want you to implement most of the changes you outlined. be critical with what you decide to include. also, i need you to write extensive e2e tests for each and every component'

contribution.description: 3-way PCA comparison analysis: public discourse (891 chunks) + artist probes (1,259) + public probes (250 Likert anchors) in the consensus semantic space. Full 31 seeds. Computed pairwise JSD, Cramer's V, centroid distances, k-NN mixing rates, and theme-specific salience ratios across all three datasets. Generated 3 publication-quality figures and metrics CSV. Key finding: artist probes and public probes are much closer to each other (JSD=0.056) than either is to public discourse (JSD>0.23), confirming H2 style-control argument.

context.conversation_snippet: User: 'make another visualization from the PCA 2d viz that includes reference + artists probes + public probes (3 data sets). do extensive statistical and comparative tests'

decision.description: Remove all em dashes from manuscript and all related documents. Em dashes (53 total) were identified as the single most visible AI-generated writing pattern. Replace parenthetical em-dash pairs with commas or separate sentences. Replace single em dashes with periods, colons, or commas.
decision.rationale: Em dashes are heavily overused by LLM-generated text (53 instances vs 5-10 in typical human academic writing). Their presence is a visible tell of AI-assisted drafting. Removing them improves naturalness and reduces the risk of reviewers or readers flagging the text as AI-generated.
decision.revision_note: Implemented. 53 em dashes removed from manuscript, 14 from repo documentation (README, supplementary, TRACE README). Zero em dashes remain across all documents.

context.conversation_snippet: User: 'i also want to ensure that there are absolutely no em dashes anywhere in any of the writing - these need to be completely removed'

contribution.description: Added DOIs to 12 journal/conference references that were missing them. Each DOI verified against publisher page (SAGE, Springer, ACM, MIT Press, Oxford, Wiley, MDPI, Frontiers, PeerJ, AAAI). Total references: 35. With DOI: 24. Without DOI: 11 (all non-journal items: websites, court filings, blog posts, working papers, master's thesis, conference proceedings without DOIs).

context.conversation_snippet: User: 'proceed to complete the DOIs for all 12 references that are missing and then confirm that all of the references have DOIs'

decision.description: Target Humanities and Social Sciences Communications Nature Collection 'Reframing Artificial Intelligence: Critical Perspectives from AI Social Science' as a second submission venue alongside NMS. Create NatureCollection2026 directory with adapted manuscript, cover letter, and pre-submission inquiry emails to guest editors Dr. Barbara Barbosa Neves and Dr. Geoffrey Mead (University of Sydney). Deadline: April 30, 2026.
decision.rationale: Collaborator (Ariya) identified the Nature Collection as a good fit. Collection scope ('how AI enters and reshapes social worlds') aligns with the paper's argument about discourse structures marginalizing affected stakeholders. The Nature portfolio brand name adds CV value. The deadline (April 30) is feasible. Manuscript requires minimal adaptation from the NMS version (framing adjustments, Nature Portfolio compliance statements).
decision.revision_note: Accepted. User directed: 'No - want to submit to the Nature collection now.' Created NatureCollection2026/ directory with full manuscript (anonymous + non-anonymous), supplementary information, cover letter, editor inquiry emails to Neves and Mead, and revision plan documenting remaining changes needed for Nature Portfolio compliance.

context.conversation_snippet: User: 'my collaborator (Ariya) is interested in submitting to a collection call for papers to Nature humanities and social sciences communications'

contribution.description: Created NatureCollection2026 submission package: manuscript (anonymous + non-anonymous .md and .docx), supplementary information (.md and .docx), cover letter (.md and .docx), pre-submission inquiry emails to Dr. Barbara Barbosa Neves and Dr. Geoffrey Mead (.md and .docx), and revision plan documenting remaining Nature Portfolio compliance changes.

context.conversation_snippet: User: 'create another folder with a modified version of the NMS manuscript that is for the Nature Collection submission'

state_change.description: Repository structure completely replaced for public release
state_change.old_value: 5 tracked files (LICENSE, README.md, 2 notebooks, 1 CSV). No tests, no package, no supplementary materials.
state_change.new_value: Full restructured repo: src/ (8 modules + Pydantic models), tests/ (114 passing), data/ (split corpus), notebooks/ (3), scripts/ (3), supplementary/ (4 tables + implementation details), trace_provenance/ (25 sessions), figures/, pyproject.toml, .gitignore
state_change.reason: Manuscript promises supplementary materials 'available in our online repository' that did not exist. Open-source release requires proper packaging, tests, documentation, and data organization.
